     The Numbers Business Report

Annapolis (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,127,992Details
International Box Office $97,000
Worldwide Box Office $17,224,992
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $30,643,262
Total $30,643,262
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It's known as one of the toughest institutions in America - a home for the best and brightest who are driven to serve their country and a training facility where only the strongest survive. It's a rarified world that, by necessity, makes or breaks tomorrow's heroes. And for local Maryland kid Jake Huard the United States Naval Academy at Annapolis has been a lifelong dream he never thought could come true. Only now that it has, he is about to realize that his battle to become the man he always wanted to be is only just beginning.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,681,171 (44.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.23 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:99.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:1,605 opening theaters/1,607 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,274,934

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 27th, 2006 (Wide) by Walt Disney
Video Release: June 27th, 2006 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence, sexual content and language.
Running Time: 83 minutes
Plot point: Boxing, Romance
Social setting: Military Life
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Touchstone Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

It's known as one of the toughest institutions in America - a home for the best and brightest who are driven to serve their country and a training facility where only the strongest survive. It's a rarified world that, by necessity, makes or breaks tomorrow's heroes. And for local Maryland kid Jake Huard the United States Naval Academy at Annapolis has been a lifelong dream he never thought could come true. Only now that it has, he is about to realize that his battle to become the man he always wanted to be is only just beginning.
